claim.observation: Volatility3 windows.netscan on the nfury memory image reports three TCP endpoints from this host to internal address 10.3.58.4: System (PID 4) 10.3.58.6:49325 -> 10.3.58.4:139 CLOSED; lsass.exe (PID 552) :49285 -> 10.3.58.4:135 CLOSED; and lsass.exe (PID 552) :49286 -> 10.3.58.4:49156 CLOSED.
claim.interpretation: Host nfury (10.3.58.6) initiated SMB/NetBIOS (139), RPC endpoint-mapper (135), and a dynamically-assigned RPC port (49156) connections to a single internal peer, 10.3.58.4. SMB plus RPC/EPM plus a high dynamic port directed from this host at one internal peer is consistent with host-to-host SMB/RPC access such as remote administration or lateral movement. The fact that lsass.exe (not a user process) owns the RPC sockets is notable. netscan residual sockets alone cannot establish intent, success, or which host was compromised first.
